llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: After re-reading the full paper, this remains a quasi-experimental difference-in-differences study comparing restaurant-level aggregated outcomes before (2022) and after (2023) Yelp introduced AI writing guidance. This is NOT a randomized controlled trial with human-only vs human+AI arms at the individual level. The 'treatment' is a time period (2023) vs 'control' time period (2022), not individual-level random assignment. The paper reports regression coefficients from DiD models with log-transformed outcomes (e.g., log(Helpfulness Votes)), not raw means, SDs, or sample sizes per arm. The sample consists of 46,190 restaurants and 1,559,528 reviews aggregated at the restaurant-week level (847,902 observations in main analysis, Table 1). No per-arm means, SDs, or individual-level sample sizes are provided. The reported statistics are DiD interaction coefficients (Treat*Post) with standard errors, not t-statistics, F-statistics, or standardized effect sizes. P-values are reported as inequalities (p < 0.001, p < 0.01, p < 0.05) throughout, not exact values. The study design does not permit computation of Hedges' g for a human-only vs human+AI comparison because there are no such arms—only time periods with all users exposed to the AI guidance after April 2023. This is a policy evaluation, not an experiment comparing AI vs no-AI conditions for the same outcome measure.
llm_extracted.notes: This is a difference-in-differences quasi-experimental study, not an RCT. The 'arms' are time periods (2022 vs 2023) at the restaurant level, not individual-level treatment assignments. The paper reports DiD regression coefficients (Treat*Post) with standard errors for log-transformed outcomes, not means/SDs for treatment vs control groups. Sample: 46,190 restaurants, 1,559,528 reviews, 847,902 restaurant-week observations (Table 1). No per-arm n, means, or SDs are provided. P-values are reported as inequalities (p < 0.001, p < 0.01, p < 0.05) throughout Tables 2-9 and Appendices, never as exact values. No t-statistics, F-statistics, or standardized effect sizes (Cohen's d, Hedges' g) are reported. The study evaluates a platform-wide policy change (AI writing guidance introduced April 2023), not a randomized comparison of AI vs no-AI conditions. Cannot compute Hedges' g from the available information. The design does not fit the schema's requirement for a human-only baseline arm and a human+AI arm with comparable outcome measures.
source_quotes: The final sample contains 46,190 restaurants and their 1,559,528 reviews. (page 6, Section 4.2)
Table 1 summary statistics: Weekly Review Volume mean 1.663, Weekly Review Length mean 441.815, Weekly Review Helpfulness Votes mean 1.377, Num. Obs. at restaurant level (page 21)
Table 6, Column 1: Treat*Post -0.037*** (0.004) for log(Helpfulness Votes), Num. Obs. 847,902 (page 29)
Table 7, Panel A (Less Experienced Users): Treat*Post -0.023*** (0.003) for log(Helpfulness Votes), Num. Obs. 683,776 (page 30)
Table 7, Panel B (Experienced Users): Treat*Post -0.072*** (0.007) for log(Helpfulness Votes), Num. Obs. 293,448 (page 30)
Note: Standard errors in parentheses are robust and clustered by restaurant. ∗ p < 0.1, ∗∗ p < 0.05, ∗∗∗ p < 0.01. (repeated in all tables)
We employ a differences-in-differences approach that compares observations from calendar year 2023 (the inception of Yelp's AI writing guidance) with the previous year 2022 (page 5, Section 5.1)
log Y_itw = β1*Treat_t * Post_w + β2*Treat_t + γ'X_itw + δ_i + λ_w + ε_itw (Equation 1, page 22)
